steps.title: Interpret the hypothesis in behavioral terms
steps.content:
The second team predicts intermediate-headed fish will be “more likely to detect” obstructions in low-visibility with large vortices.

Detecting an obstruction means avoiding it, so better detection = fewer collisions.

A supporting finding would show intermediate-headed fish bumping into fewer obstructions than wide-headed fish.

steps.title: Find the result that demonstrates the predicted difference
steps.content:
Choice C presents this directly: wide-headed fish bumped into obstructions more often than intermediate-headed fish in the exact conditions (large vortices, low-visibility).

A, B, and D each fail to demonstrate this comparison—one presents only a single fish type, one compares individuals within a type rather than between types, and one shows equal performance across types.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
